metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2336
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.4: "TrkC and PTPσ Ectodomains Induce Excitatory Presynaptic and Postsynaptic Differentiation, Respectively"
Fig.5: "Aggregation of TrkC or PTPσ on the Neuron Surface Mediates Postsynaptic and Presynaptic Differentiation, Respectively"
Fig.6: "Blockade of TrkC-PTPσ Interaction with a Neutralizing Antibody Inhibits Synaptogenic Activity of TrkC and PTPσ and Reduces Excitatory Synapse Density"

The model is that presynaptic PTPσ clusters postsynaptic TrkC which in turn instructs postsynapse assembly.. Experimental description: Experimental Procedures
Cell Cultures, Transfection, and Immunocytochemistry
Cultures of hippocampal neurons, neuron-fibroblast cocultures, and immunocytochemistry were performed essentially as described (Linhoff et al., 2009).

M.W. Linhoff, J. Lauren, R.M. Cassidy, F.A. Dobie, H. Takahashi, H.B. Nygaard, M.S. Airaksinen, S.M. Strittmatter, A.M. Craig
An unbiased expression screen for synaptogenic proteins identifies the LRRTM protein family as synaptic organizers. Neuron, 61 (2009)
Experimental Procedures
Cell Culture
Cultures of hippocampal neurons were prepared from E18 rat embryos.

- Overexpressed proteins were of rat species: "The cDNAs for full-length rat TrkCTK- (BC078844), TrkCTK+ (NM_019248), TrkCKI25 (AAB26724.1), TrkA (NM_021589), TrkBTK- (NM_001163168), TrkBTK+ (NM_012731), p75NTR (NM_012610) were cloned by RT-PCR from a P11 rat brain cDNA library (Linhoff et al., 2009)..."
